findings.one_line: This lemma is marked proved but its proof only cites classical Le Cam/Bretagnolle-Huber testing inequalities; with no declared dependency or in-core derivation, the constant-error testing step used in the lower bound is not discharged from the DAG.

The feasible upper bound is stated as a supremum over `def:law-class`, but the proof relies on extra nuisance-rate/exponent and localized-envelope conditions that are not part of that class and are not derived from primitive class assumptions, so the proved rate is only for a narrower scoped regime as written.

This node presents a two-sided characterization of `M_n(alpha,gamma)`, but its upper half inherits `oeq:feasible-upper`'s additional feasible-side conditions while the lower half is over the base law class, so the delivered content is a converse on `def:law-class` plus an achievability result for a stricter setup rather than a single-class rate characterization.
